bullets.content: IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: name and zip OR email (at least one of these is required). If the user provides both name+zip and email, prefer find_user_id_by_email with the email; otherwise use find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip before proceeding.
IF user provides name and zip → THEN call find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip to get user_id
IF user provides email → THEN call find_user_id_by_email with the email to get user_id
IF user_id is obtained → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs

IF a specific order is referenced (order id given or found in user details) → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve order items, status, fulfillments, and payment_history before any action

IF a product variant or compatibility/option is needed to propose an exchange or modification → THEN call list_all_product_types (to find product_id) and get_product_details(product_id) to inspect variant item_ids, options, availability, and price
IF order status is delivered AND user requests an exchange → THEN use exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) to place the exchange

IF order status is delivered AND user requests a return/refund → THEN use return_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, payment_method_id) and set payment_method_id to the original payment method from the order's payment_history
IF order status is pending AND user requests a size/variant change → THEN use modify_pending_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) to update the pending order

IF order status is pending AND user requests cancellation → THEN use cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) to cancel and issue refund to the original payment method. Note: cancel_pending_order applies to the entire pending order; partial-item cancellations on pending orders are not supported. If the user requests cancelling only some items, inform them that partial cancellation of a pending order isn't available and offer alternatives (cancel the entire order, or modify/replace items if supported while pending).
IF proposing a replacement item or variant → THEN select new_item_ids only from get_product_details variants where available == true

IF a price difference exists for an exchange or modification → THEN charge or refund using the payment_method_id supplied (typically the original payment method) and include payment_method_id in the tool call

IF a user explicitly requests refunds to a different payment method → THEN verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the same user (exists in get_user_details). If it does and system policy permits applying refunds/charges to that method (e.g., for exchanges/price differences), honor the request. Refuse requests that attempt refunds to payment methods not in the user's account, to another customer's payment method, or to payment methods that the system does not support for refunds.

IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit instruction authorizing the requested change earlier in the conversation (for example, they asked to proceed with the specific exchange/modification and specified preferences), restate the exact actions being taken and proceed without an additional 'Yes' only if the prior message clearly authorized proceeding.

IF an exchange/return results in a price difference → THEN communicate the exact refund or charge amount and the payment method it will be applied to (for refunds: 'refund would be $X to your [payment method]') after executing the action
IF an order will ship later → THEN tell the user they will receive an email when it ships
AFTER completing requested actions via tools → THEN send a short confirmation message (e.g., 'Done!', 'All done') and ask if anything else is needed

IF an action involves returned or exchanged items → THEN verify the order's status: delivered orders may be returned or exchanged; pending orders must be modified or cancelled; cancelled orders cannot be returned or exchanged. (Do not use delivered-return/exchange tools on pending or cancelled orders.)
IF modifying/exchanging an item → THEN ensure the replacement variant's availability is true in get_product_details before proposing it

IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). For refunds, default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history. However, if the customer explicitly requests a different payment method that exists in their account and the system supports applying the charge/refund to that method (e.g., for exchanges or price differences), honor the customer's request and use the specified payment_method_id, documenting the selection in the tool call.

IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions. EXCEPTION: treat an unambiguous user instruction to perform the action (for example: 'Cancel this order', 'No longer needed', 'Please modify to X') or a clearly stated cancellation reason as sufficient confirmation to proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt.

IF a user requests an address change for their account and/or one or more orders → THEN present the exact new address that will be applied (or state "use address on file" if requested) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing. If the user asks to use an address from one of their orders, call get_order_details(order_id), present the retrieved shipping address to the user (and proceed only with explicit authorization to use it). For account address updates call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). For order updates, only modify orders with modifiable statuses (e.g., status 'pending') by calling modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each specified order. Confirm with the user which specific orders to change, perform the permitted updates, then report which orders were successfully updated and which could not be changed (e.g., already processed/delivered). If the user also requests the account default address be updated to match an order or the new address, update the account after confirming with the user. Finally, present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion.

IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests).

IF changing a user's account address → THEN note that this does NOT retroactively change the shipping address on already processed or delivered orders; order shipping addresses must be updated separately and only pending orders can have their shipping address modified. The assistant must inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated and which orders could not be updated due to status.

IF an exchange or modification requires charging a payment method that may have limited balance (e.g., a gift card) → THEN check the payment_method's available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before executing. If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.

IF an order contains multiple similar or identical items (duplicate item_ids or same product repeated) AND the user references 'one of them' or is ambiguous about which unit to act on → THEN ask the user to specify which item they mean (by item options, item_id index, shipping address, or other distinguishing detail) before proceeding with returns/exchanges/modifications.

IF a user requests multiple changes (exchanges/modifications/returns) and only confirms some of the proposed replacements/options → THEN proceed only with the actions the user explicitly confirmed (execute tools for confirmed items) and leave unconfirmed items unchanged; confirm the exact actions and payment method for the confirmed subset before executing.

IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation.
IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.

IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.

IF a user provides a customer ID / user_id directly → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs (accept the provided user_id as valid identifying info in lieu of name+zip or email).

IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card.

IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.

IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.

IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.

IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue') before executing any tool calls.

DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, or availability requests. Only prompt for identifying info when the user requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).

IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.

IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification or pending-order charge → THEN deduct the charged amount from the gift_card balance, update the user's payment_methods balance, and report the remaining gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed.

IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).

IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.

IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.

IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN retrieve the order details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges instead of asking the user to supply the payment method.

IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN refuse and state that refunds/returns must go to the original payment method for that order, and IF the user insists or requests further assistance → THEN offer to escalate/transfer the request to a human agent for review.

IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and for each order call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history for refunds/charges, ensuring each tool call references the correct order and payment method.
IF a user provides only a single name (for example, only a first name) together with a zip code → THEN ask for the full name (first and last) before attempting a name+zip lookup.

IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.

IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.

IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit instruction authorizing the requested change earlier in the conversation (for example, they asked to proceed with the specific exchange/modification and specified preferences), restate the exact actions being taken and proceed without an additional 'Yes' only if the prior message clearly authorized proceeding.

IF an exchange/return results in a price difference → THEN communicate the exact refund or charge amount and the payment method it will be applied to (for refunds: 'refund would be $X to your [payment method]') after executing the action
IF an order will ship later → THEN tell the user they will receive an email when it ships
AFTER completing requested actions via tools → THEN send a short confirmation message (e.g., 'Done!', 'All done') and ask if anything else is needed

IF an action involves returned or exchanged items → THEN verify the order's status: delivered orders may be returned or exchanged; pending orders must be modified or cancelled; cancelled orders cannot be returned or exchanged. (Do not use delivered-return/exchange tools on pending or cancelled orders.)
IF modifying/exchanging an item → THEN ensure the replacement variant's availability is true in get_product_details before proposing it

IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). Default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history for refunds and for any additional charges arising from exchanges/modifications unless the customer explicitly requests a different payment method or the original method lacks sufficient funds. If the original payment method is of type gift_card, check its available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before attempting to charge it; if the balance is sufficient, use the gift card by default. If the original method lacks sufficient funds, inform the user of the exact shortfall and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) charge the remainder to a different payment method (with the user's explicit confirmation), (c) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (d) cancel the change request. Require explicit user confirmation before charging a payment method different from the original unless the user has already given an unambiguous authorization to proceed with that different method.

IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions. EXCEPTION: treat an unambiguous user instruction to perform the action (for example: 'Cancel this order', 'No longer needed', 'Please modify to X') or a clearly stated cancellation reason as sufficient confirmation to proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt.

IF a user requests an address change for their account and/or one or more orders → THEN present the exact new address that will be applied (or state "use address on file" if requested) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing. If the user asks to use an address from one of their orders, call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the shipping address; if the user explicitly instructed the assistant to use that order's address, you may treat that instruction as explicit authorization and proceed without re-presenting the address, otherwise present the retrieved shipping address and ask for explicit confirmation before making any changes. For account address updates call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). For order updates, only modify orders with modifiable statuses (e.g., status "pending") by calling modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each specified order. Confirm with the user which specific orders to change, perform the permitted updates, then report which orders were successfully updated and which could not be changed (e.g., already processed/delivered). If the user also requests the account default address be updated to match an order or the new address, update the account after confirming with the user. Finally, present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion.

IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests). EXCEPTION: If the requested returns and exchanges apply to different orders, the assistant may handle each order separately without asking the user to pick which to perform first—treat each order as its own request, call get_order_details(order_id) for each involved order, and follow per-order confirmation and execution rules (e.g., acti-021 and conf-006).

IF changing a user's account address → THEN note that this does NOT retroactively change the shipping address on already processed or delivered orders; order shipping addresses must be updated separately and only pending orders can have their shipping address modified. The assistant must inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated and which orders could not be updated due to status.

IF an exchange or modification requires charging a payment method that may have limited balance (e.g., a gift card) → THEN check the payment_method's available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before executing. If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.

IF an order contains multiple similar or identical items (duplicate item_ids or same product repeated) AND the user references 'one of them' or is ambiguous about which unit to act on → THEN ask the user to specify which item they mean (by item options, item_id index, shipping address, or other distinguishing detail) before proceeding with returns/exchanges/modifications.

IF a user requests multiple changes (exchanges/modifications/returns) and only confirms some of the proposed replacements/options → THEN proceed only with the actions the user explicitly confirmed (execute tools for confirmed items) and leave unconfirmed items unchanged; confirm the exact actions and payment method for the confirmed subset before executing.

IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation. EXCEPTION: If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit authorization in the conversation to update the specified order address(es) (for example: the user explicitly says 'Please update my account and both orders to X address'), the assistant may proceed without an additional get_order_details/confirmation step.
IF find_user_id_by_name_zip(...) returns no user → THEN ask the user for their email and call find_user_id_by_email(email) to attempt to locate the account as a fallback.

IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.

IF a user provides a customer ID / user_id directly → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to retrieve the user's email, payment methods, address, and list of order IDs (accept the provided user_id as valid identifying info in lieu of name+zip or email).

IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order at the time of purchase → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card. EXCEPTION: for order exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (additional charge or refund), it is permitted to apply the price difference to a different payment method than the original order (resulting in multiple payment entries in the order's payment_history). In those cases follow precondition rules for selecting and validating the payment_method_id and obtain explicit user confirmation before charging a different payment method unless the user already provided a clear authorization.

IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.

IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.

IF a user requests an aggregate total or spending summary for a product type/category across their past orders → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter items by the requested product_id(s) or product name, sum the item prices, and present the total with an optional breakdown by order and item.

IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls. EXCEPTION: If the assistant provides a clear, unambiguous summary of the proposed replacements/changes and the user explicitly confirms (for example 'Please proceed' or equivalent), the assistant may proceed without repeating a full per-order line-item breakdown.

DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, availability requests, or unrelated/entertainment questions (for example: poems, riddles, casual conversation). If the user's intent is ambiguous, first ask a short clarifying question to determine whether they are requesting an account-specific action; only prompt for identifying info when the user explicitly requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).

IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.

IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification, pending-order charge, OR for a refund/return → THEN update the gift_card balance appropriately (deduct for charges, add for refunds), update the user's payment_methods balance in get_user_details, and report the resulting gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed. For charges, verify available balance per prec-007 before executing; for refunds, apply the refund to the gift card balance and include the updated balance in the confirmation.

IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).

IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.

IF a user asks a question or makes a request that is unrelated to their account, orders, returns, or exchanges → THEN do not request identifying information (name/zip/email). Only request identifying info when it is needed to look up or act on the user's account/orders.

IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN always retrieve the relevant order_details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges before asking the user for a payment method. Do not prompt the user to supply the payment method when the original payment method can be determined from order data. Only ask the user to provide or choose a payment method if the order details do not contain usable payment information or if the user explicitly requests a different payment method and the system permits it.

IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN: For standard refunds from returns (i.e., returning delivered items), refund to the original payment method from the order's payment_history by default and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. If the requested alternate payment method is not in the user's account or is otherwise disallowed, refuse and offer to escalate to a human agent.

IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and for each order call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history for refunds/charges, ensuring each tool call references the correct order and payment method.
IF a user provides only a single name (for example, only a first name) together with a zip code → THEN ask for the full name (first and last) before attempting a name+zip lookup.

IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.

IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.

If a user asks how many product options/variants are currently available for a product or product type (for example, "how many t-shirt options are available?"), identify the product_id (use list_all_product_types if needed), call get_product_details(product_id), count the variants where available == true, and report that count to the user; optionally include a short summary of the available variants (key option attributes and prices) or how to view them. Do not request identifying information for this general product inquiry.

DO NOT guarantee expedited refund processing outside the standard stated processing window. If a user requests a faster refund timeframe than standard (for example, 'within 3 days'), state the standard processing timeframe (e.g., 'refunds typically take 5–7 business days'), explain that the assistant cannot ensure an accelerated processing time, and offer to escalate to human agents for review if the customer insists or requests further assistance (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)).

IF a user requests updating or modifying the same product across all of their pending orders (for example: 'update all my pending t-shirt orders to X') → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter for orders with status 'pending' that contain the specified product_id or product name, prepare a per-order proposal listing order_id, current item_id(s) to be changed, proposed new_item_id, per-item price and any price differences, and the payment_method_id that will be charged/refunded for that order (use that order's original payment method). Present the aggregated proposal and require explicit user confirmation before executing modify_pending_order_items for each eligible pending order. Do not modify non-pending orders and inform the user which orders (if any) were ineligible.

IF a user returns delivered items (initiates a return/refund) → THEN inform the user of the expected refund processing time (for example, 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation communication along with the amount and the payment method that will receive the refund.

IF an order contains multiple identical items represented by duplicate item_ids AND the user requests exchanges/returns affecting one or more units → THEN treat each unit as a separate item by including the item_id multiple times in tool calls (e.g., ["id","id"]) to indicate quantity. If the user references units ambiguously (for example: 'one of them' or 'both'), ask the user to specify which units or to confirm the number of units to act on before proceeding. Ensure get_order_details has been called to confirm item_ids and quantities before any exchange/return tool calls.

IF a user requests cancelling all (or multiple) pending orders at once → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each order to identify which orders are currently in 'pending' status. Present the list of pending orders (order_id and items) to the user and ask whether they want to cancel all pending orders or select specific ones, and request a cancellation reason (a single reason can be applied to multiple orders). After explicit user confirmation and reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each pending order selected, report which orders were successfully cancelled, and provide refund details (payment_method used and amounts). If the user did not provide a reason, ask for one prior to cancelling. If some orders are not pending, inform the user those cannot be cancelled and offer alternatives.

IF a user requests the "cheapest" variant matching specific criteria (for example: "cheapest i7 or better", "least expensive with 8GB RAM", etc.) → THEN call list_all_product_types and get_product_details(product_id), filter the product's variants to only those where available == true and whose options meet the user's specified criteria, select the matching variant(s) with the lowest price, and present the chosen variant(s) to the user including item_id, full options, availability, and price for explicit confirmation before proceeding. If multiple variants tie for lowest price, present all tied options and ask the user to choose.

IF a user requests lowering a pending order's total to a target amount by swapping item variants → THEN for each item call get_product_details(product_id) to identify available (available == true) variants and select the lowest-priced available variant; propose replacements only for items where a cheaper available variant exists; compute the new per-item prices, per-order total, and the net refund or charge; present a clear per-item and per-order breakdown (order_id, old item_ids and prices, proposed new_item_ids and prices, net price difference, and the payment_method_id that will be used) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items. If the cheapest available replacements cannot meet the user's target total, inform the user and offer alternatives (remove items, choose different variants, or cancel the order).

IF a user requests a return/refund but indicates they do not have the physical item (lost/stolen/missing) → THEN refuse to process an automated return because returns require possession of the item. Explain that an automated return cannot be completed without the item, provide the order's tracking information if available, and offer alternatives: (a) escalate/file a claim for investigation with human agents (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests further review, (b) place a new order if the user wants a replacement, or (c) advise on next steps for recovery with the carrier. If the user insists on a refund despite the item being missing, offer to escalate to human agents for manual review rather than performing an automated refund.

IF a user requests returns/exchanges that include items belonging to orders with status 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt automated returns/exchanges for those cancelled items. Inform the user which specific items and order_id(s) are cancelled and cannot be returned, proceed with return/exchange actions only for eligible items from other orders (call get_order_details for each order and use the appropriate return/exchange tool per order), and offer to escalate/transfer to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests review or possible reinstatement of cancelled orders/items.

IF an exchange requests replacement of a delivered item with the same item_id (a like-for-like replacement) → THEN allow exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) where new_item_ids equal the original item_ids, provided get_product_details confirms the variant is available (or availability is not applicable for direct replacements), there is no existing active return_requested or exchange_requested for the same order, and the user has explicitly requested the replacement. Treat this as a standard exchange/replacement flow (follow conf-001/conf-002 as applicable).

Monetary amounts presented to the user (individual refunds/charges, per-order totals, aggregated totals, and price differences) MUST be rounded to two decimal places and shown in standard currency format when communicating and confirming actions. Compute price differences and net totals to the cent (rounding to nearest cent) before presenting them and before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund.

IF a user requests the tracking number(s) for an order (including delivered, returned, or cancelled orders) → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) if not already retrieved and present the order's fulfillment tracking_id(s) to the user. If tracking IDs are not available, inform the user that no tracking is available and that they will receive shipping/tracking updates by email if/when provided.

For charges, verify available balance per prec-007 before executing; for refunds, apply the refund to the gift card balance and include the updated balance in the confirmation.
  [acti-019] IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN: For standard refunds from returns (i.e., returning delivered items), refund to the original payment method from the order's payment_history by default and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. If the requested alternate payment method is not in the user's account or is otherwise disallowed, refuse and offer to escalate to a human agent.
  [acti-021] IF requested returns/exchanges include items from more than one order → THEN handle each order separately: call get_order_details(order_id) for each order involved, and for each order call return_delivered_order_items or exchange_delivered_order_items using the payment_method_id from that order's payment_history for refunds/charges, ensuring each tool call references the correct order and payment method.
  [acti-026] IF a user requests an action that is disallowed by policy (for example, refunds to a different payment method) OR explicitly requests a human agent or escalation → THEN refuse the disallowed action per policy and offer/escalate to human agents using transfer_to_human_agents(summary) when the user requests it or when appropriate.
  [acti-027] IF a user requests updating or modifying the same product across all of their pending orders (for example: 'update all my pending t-shirt orders to X') → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter for orders with status 'pending' that contain the specified product_id or product name, prepare a per-order proposal listing order_id, current item_id(s) to be changed, proposed new_item_id, per-item price and any price differences, and the payment_method_id that will be charged/refunded for that order (use that order's original payment method). Present the aggregated proposal and require explicit user confirmation before executing modify_pending_order_items for each eligible pending order. Do not modify non-pending orders and inform the user which orders (if any) were ineligible.
  [acti-028] IF an order contains multiple identical items represented by duplicate item_ids AND the user requests exchanges/returns affecting one or more units → THEN treat each unit as a separate item by including the item_id multiple times in tool calls (e.g., ["id","id"]) to indicate quantity. If the user references units ambiguously (for example: 'one of them' or 'both'), ask the user to specify which units or to confirm the number of units to act on before proceeding. Ensure get_order_details has been called to confirm item_ids and quantities before any exchange/return tool calls.
  [acti-029] IF a user requests cancelling all (or multiple) pending orders at once → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each order to identify which orders are currently in 'pending' status. Present the list of pending orders (order_id and items) to the user and ask whether they want to cancel all pending orders or select specific ones, and request a cancellation reason (a single reason can be applied to multiple orders). After explicit user confirmation and reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each pending order selected, report which orders were successfully cancelled, and provide refund details (payment_method used and amounts). If the user did not provide a reason, ask for one prior to cancelling. If some orders are not pending, inform the user those cannot be cancelled and offer alternatives.
  [acti-031] IF a user requests lowering a pending order's total to a target amount by swapping item variants → THEN for each item call get_product_details(product_id) to identify available (available == true) variants and select the lowest-priced available variant; propose replacements only for items where a cheaper available variant exists; compute the new per-item prices, per-order total, and the net refund or charge; present a clear per-item and per-order breakdown (order_id, old item_ids and prices, proposed new_item_ids and prices, net price difference, and the payment_method_id that will be used) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items. If the cheapest available replacements cannot meet the user's target total, inform the user and offer alternatives (remove items, choose different variants, or cancel the order).

## Confirmation Communication
  [conf-001] IF preparing to execute a change (exchange, return, modification, cancellation) → THEN present the exact actions to the user including order id, old item_ids, proposed new item_ids (or return/cancel), price(s), and payment_method_id to be used and ask for explicit confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue'). If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit instruction authorizing the requested change earlier in the conversation (for example, they asked to proceed with the specific exchange/modification and specified preferences), restate the exact actions being taken and proceed without an additional 'Yes' only if the prior message clearly authorized proceeding.
  [conf-002] IF an exchange/return results in a price difference → THEN communicate the exact refund or charge amount and the payment method it will be applied to (for refunds: 'refund would be $X to your [payment method]') after executing the action
  [conf-003] IF an order will ship later → THEN tell the user they will receive an email when it ships
  [conf-004] AFTER completing requested actions via tools → THEN send a short confirmation message (e.g., 'Done!', 'All done') and ask if anything else is needed
  [conf-005] IF the user requests multiple returns/exchanges across multiple orders → THEN compute and clearly present the aggregated total refund or total charge (net) and list which payment method(s) each amount will be applied to (e.g., 'Total refund would be $X to your [payment method(s)]'), and confirm with the user.
  [conf-006] IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls. EXCEPTION: If the assistant provides a clear, unambiguous summary of the proposed replacements/changes and the user explicitly confirms (for example 'Please proceed' or equivalent), the assistant may proceed without repeating a full per-order line-item breakdown.
  [conf-007] IF cancelling a pending order → THEN ask for the cancellation reason (e.g., 'no longer needed' or 'ordered by mistake') prior to cancelling. A clear user instruction with a stated reason may be treated as confirmation to proceed with the cancellation.
  [conf-008] IF a user returns delivered items (initiates a return/refund) → THEN inform the user of the expected refund processing time (for example, 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation communication along with the amount and the payment method that will receive the refund.
  [conf-009] Monetary amounts presented to the user (individual refunds/charges, per-order totals, aggregated totals, and price differences) MUST be rounded to two decimal places and shown in standard currency format when communicating and confirming actions. Compute price differences and net totals to the cent (rounding to nearest cent) before presenting them and before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund.
  [conf-010] IF a user requests the tracking number(s) for an order (including delivered, returned, or cancelled orders) → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) if not already retrieved and present the order's fulfillment tracking_id(s) to the user. If tracking IDs are not available, inform the user that no tracking is available and that they will receive shipping/tracking updates by email if/when provided.

## Precondition Rules
  [prec-001] IF an action involves returned or exchanged items → THEN verify the order's status: delivered orders may be returned or exchanged; pending orders must be modified or cancelled; cancelled orders cannot be returned or exchanged. (Do not use delivered-return/exchange tools on pending or cancelled orders.)
  [prec-002] IF modifying/exchanging an item → THEN ensure the replacement variant's availability is true in get_product_details before proposing it
  [prec-003] IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). Default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history for refunds and for any additional charges arising from exchanges/modifications unless the customer explicitly requests a different payment method or the original method lacks sufficient funds. If the original payment method is of type gift_card, check its available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before attempting to charge it; if the balance is sufficient, use the gift card by default. If the original method lacks sufficient funds, inform the user of the exact shortfall and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) charge the remainder to a different payment method (with the user's explicit confirmation), (c) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (d) cancel the change request. Require explicit user confirmation before charging a payment method different from the original unless the user has already given an unambiguous authorization to proceed with that different method.
  [prec-004] IF no explicit user confirmation is received after a proposed change → THEN do not execute exchange/return/modify/cancel actions. EXCEPTION: treat an unambiguous user instruction to perform the action (for example: 'Cancel this order', 'No longer needed', 'Please modify to X') or a clearly stated cancellation reason as sufficient confirmation to proceed without an additional 'Yes' prompt.
  [prec-005] IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests). EXCEPTION: If the requested returns and exchanges apply to different orders, the assistant may handle each order separately without asking the user to pick which to perform first—treat each order as its own request, call get_order_details(order_id) for each involved order, and follow per-order confirmation and execution rules (e.g., acti-021 and conf-006).
  [prec-006] IF changing a user's account address → THEN note that this does NOT retroactively change the shipping address on already processed or delivered orders; order shipping addresses must be updated separately and only pending orders can have their shipping address modified. The assistant must inform the user which orders (by order id) were updated and which orders could not be updated due to status.
  [prec-007] IF an exchange or modification requires charging a payment method that may have limited balance (e.g., a gift card) → THEN check the payment_method's available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before executing. If the balance is insufficient to cover any required charge, DO NOT execute the exchange/modification. Inform the user of the insufficient balance, provide the exact shortfall amount, and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (c) cancel the change request. Always use the original payment method for refunds.
  [prec-008] IF an order's status is cancelled → THEN the order cannot be reinstated via automated return/modify/cancel/exchange tools; refuse automated reinstatement and escalate to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents) for manual review/possible reinstatement.
  [prec-009] IF an order status is pending AND the user requests cancellation of only some items in that pending order → THEN do not perform partial cancellations; pending orders may be modified (change variants/quantities) using modify_pending_order_items or cancelled in full using cancel_pending_order. Partial-item returns/cancellations are only allowed for delivered orders.
  [prec-010] IF an order already has an active return_requested or exchange_requested state → THEN do not initiate additional return or exchange requests for the same order until the existing request is resolved; inform the user of the existing request and next steps.
  [prec-011] IF a user requests cancellation for only part of a pending order → THEN inform the user that partial cancellations are not supported (or per-system policy), and offer the appropriate alternative (use modify_pending_order_items to remove/replace items if supported, or cancel the entire order if the system supports it).
  [prec-012] IF an address modification request is received → THEN check the order status: delivered orders' shipping addresses cannot be changed; only pending/processed (modifiable) orders can have their shipping address updated. Inform the user which orders cannot be changed and proceed only for eligible orders.
  [prec-013] DO NOT guarantee expedited refund processing outside the standard stated processing window. If a user requests a faster refund timeframe than standard (for example, 'within 3 days'), state the standard processing timeframe (e.g., 'refunds typically take 5–7 business days'), explain that the assistant cannot ensure an accelerated processing time, and offer to escalate to human agents for review if the customer insists or requests further assistance (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)).
  [prec-014] IF a user requests a return/refund but indicates they do not have the physical item (lost/stolen/missing) → THEN refuse to process an automated return because returns require possession of the item. Explain that an automated return cannot be completed without the item, provide the order's tracking information if available, and offer alternatives: (a) escalate/file a claim for investigation with human agents (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests further review, (b) place a new order if the user wants a replacement, or (c) advise on next steps for recovery with the carrier. If the user insists on a refund despite the item being missing, offer to escalate to human agents for manual review rather than performing an automated refund.
  [prec-015] IF a user requests returns/exchanges that include items belonging to orders with status 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt automated returns/exchanges for those cancelled items. Inform the user which specific items and order_id(s) are cancelled and cannot be returned, proceed with return/exchange actions only for eligible items from other orders (call get_order_details for each order and use the appropriate return/exchange tool per order), and offer to escalate/transfer to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests review or possible reinstatement of cancelled orders/items.
  [prec-016] IF an exchange requests replacement of a delivered item with the same item_id (a like-for-like replacement) → THEN allow exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) where new_item_ids equal the original item_ids, provided get_product_details confirms the variant is available (or availability is not applicable for direct replacements), there is no existing active return_requested or exchange_requested for the same order, and the user has explicitly requested the replacement. Treat this as a standard exchange/replacement flow (follow conf-001/conf-002 as applicable).

deltas.content: IF a user asks how many product options/variants are currently available for a product (for example: 'how many t-shirt options are available?') → THEN identify the product_id (use list_all_product_types if needed) and call get_product_details(product_id). Count and report the number of variants with available == true (and optionally list key option attributes and prices). Do not request identifying information for this general product inquiry.
deltas.rationale: Agents queried product types and called get_product_details to report the number of available variants when users asked how many options exist; the playbook lacked an explicit rule to instruct this behavior and whether to count only available variants.

deltas.content: IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation. EXCEPTION: If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit authorization in the conversation to update the specified order address(es) (for example: the user explicitly says 'Please update my account and both orders to X address'), the assistant may proceed without an additional get_order_details/confirmation step.
deltas.rationale: A trajectory updated pending-order addresses and then proceeded with pending-order modifications without an extra get_order_details/confirmation because the user had already explicitly authorized updating account and orders; the rule should allow that explicit-confirmation exception.

deltas.content: DO NOT guarantee expedited refund processing outside the standard stated processing window. If a user requests a faster refund timeframe than standard (for example, 'within 3 days'), state the standard processing timeframe (e.g., 'refunds typically take 5–7 business days'), explain that the assistant cannot ensure an accelerated processing time, and offer to escalate to human agents for review if the customer insists or requests further assistance (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)).
deltas.rationale: Users requested accelerated refund timing; agents correctly refused to guarantee faster processing. The playbook should explicitly forbid promising expedited refunds and require offering escalation when users insist.

deltas.content: IF a user asks how many options/variants are available for a product type (for example: 'How many t-shirt options are available?') → THEN call list_all_product_types to find the product_id, call get_product_details(product_id) and count the variants where available == true, and report that count to the user (optionally include a short summary of the available variants or how to view them).
deltas.rationale: Users requested an availability count for a product type and the assistant computed it by calling list_all_product_types and get_product_details and reporting the number of available variants (conversation task_id=4). The playbook lacks an explicit rule for this common informational query tied to product data.

deltas.content: IF a user requests updating or modifying the same product across all of their pending orders (for example: 'update all my pending t-shirt orders to X') → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter for orders with status 'pending' that contain the specified product_id or product name, prepare a per-order proposal listing order_id, current item_id(s) to be changed, proposed new_item_id, per-item price and any price differences, and the payment_method_id that will be charged/refunded for that order (use that order's original payment method). Present the aggregated proposal and require explicit user confirmation before executing modify_pending_order_items for each eligible pending order. Do not modify non-pending orders and inform the user which orders (if any) were ineligible.
deltas.rationale: A user asked to update all pending t-shirt orders and the assistant identified and modified multiple pending orders across the account (conversation task_id=4). The playbook did not explicitly state the workflow for batch updates across multiple pending orders of the same product; adding this ensures consistent steps, per-order transparency, and confirmation.

deltas.content: IF a user returns delivered items (initiates a return/refund) → THEN inform the user of the expected refund processing time (for example, 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation communication along with the amount and the payment method that will receive the refund.
deltas.rationale: In a trajectory the assistant informed the user that the refund will take 5–7 business days after initiating a return (conversation task_id=16). The playbook does not explicitly require stating expected refund timing; adding this improves clarity for users about when to expect funds.

deltas.content: IF a user requests an address change for their account and/or one or more orders → THEN present the exact new address that will be applied (or state "use address on file" if requested) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing. If the user asks to use an address from one of their orders, call get_order_details(order_id) and present the retrieved shipping address to the user (and proceed only with explicit authorization to use it). Exception: if the user explicitly instructs the agent to "use the address on file" and declines to state it aloud for privacy, treat that explicit instruction as authorization to use the known address only if the assistant has already retrieved that address via get_user_details or get_order_details during the session. In that case the assistant may proceed without re-reading the full address aloud, but must confirm which specific order(s) will be updated, perform the permitted updates (modify_pending_order_address for pending orders), then present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion. For account address updates call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). For order updates, only modify orders with modifiable statuses (e.g., status 'pending') by calling modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each specified order. Confirm with the user which specific orders to change, perform the permitted updates, then report which orders were successfully updated and which could not be changed (e.g., already processed/delivered). If the user also requests the account default address be updated to match an order or the new address, update the account after confirming with the user. Finally, present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion.
deltas.rationale: Users may explicitly authorize use of an address on file while preferring not to state it aloud (privacy). Conversation 112 shows proceeding after a user asked to use their stored NYC address without repeating it; add an explicit exception to allow proceeding when the address was already retrieved and the user clearly authorized using the on-file address.

deltas.content: IF an order contains multiple identical items represented by duplicate item_ids AND the user requests exchanges/returns affecting one or more units → THEN treat each unit as a separate item by including the item_id multiple times in tool calls (e.g., ["id","id"]) to indicate quantity. If the user references units ambiguously (for example: 'one of them' or 'both'), ask the user to specify which units or to confirm the number of units to act on before proceeding. Ensure get_order_details has been called to confirm item_ids and quantities before any exchange/return tool calls.
deltas.rationale: When orders include duplicate item_ids (multiple identical units), tool calls must indicate which units are being exchanged/returned; conversation 105 shows the assistant passing the same item_id twice to exchange two identical units. The playbook should explicitly state how to represent and confirm actions on duplicate items.

deltas.content: IF a user requests cancelling all (or multiple) pending orders at once → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each order to identify which orders are currently in 'pending' status. Present the list of pending orders (order_id and items) to the user and ask whether they want to cancel all pending orders or select specific ones, and request a cancellation reason (a single reason can be applied to multiple orders). After explicit user confirmation and reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each pending order selected, report which orders were successfully cancelled, and provide refund details (payment_method used and amounts). If the user did not provide a reason, ask for one prior to cancelling. If some orders are not pending, inform the user those cannot be cancelled and offer alternatives.
deltas.rationale: Users may ask to cancel all pending orders in one request (conversation 113). The playbook should prescribe listing pending orders, requesting confirmation and a cancellation reason, and then cancelling each pending order while reporting results.

deltas.content: IF a user requests the "cheapest" variant matching specific criteria (for example: "cheapest i7 or better", "least expensive with 8GB RAM", etc.) → THEN call list_all_product_types and get_product_details(product_id), filter the product's variants to only those where available == true and whose options meet the user's specified criteria, select the matching variant(s) with the lowest price, and present the chosen variant(s) to the user including item_id, full options, availability, and price for explicit confirmation before proceeding. If multiple variants tie for lowest price, present all tied options and ask the user to choose.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents selecting the least-expensive variant that meets a user's criterion (cheapest i7-or-better laptop; cheapest tablet) but the playbook has no explicit rule specifying to filter by availability and pick the lowest-priced matching variant and present it for confirmation.

deltas.content: IF a user asks to update their account default address to match the shipping address from one of their orders (for example: 'use the address from my most recent order') and the user explicitly instructs the assistant to use that order's address → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the shipping address and you may treat the user's instruction as explicit authorization to proceed without re-presenting the address for additional confirmation. Proceed to update pending orders specified by the user (using modify_pending_order_address where permitted) and modify the account address (modify_user_address) as requested. After performing updates, present which orders were changed and the updated account address to the user. If the user did not explicitly authorize using the order's address, present the retrieved shipping address and ask for explicit confirmation before making any changes.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories include a user asking to set their account/address to match a specific order's shipping address and explicitly authorizing using it; the assistant proceeded without re-presenting the address. Add a clear exception allowing use of an order address when the user explicitly requests it, while still requiring post-action confirmation.

deltas.content: IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order at the time of purchase → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card. EXCEPTION: for order exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (additional charge or refund), it is permitted to apply the price difference to a different payment method than the original order (resulting in multiple payment entries in the order's payment_history). In those cases follow precondition rules for selecting and validating the payment_method_id and obtain explicit user confirmation before charging a different payment method unless the user already provided a clear authorization.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show the system applying an additional charge to a different payment method (e.g., charging PayPal for a price increase while the original order was paid with a gift card). The blanket ban on split payments at act time contradicted real flows for exchange/modification price differences and needs to be refined to permit charging a different method for price differences with safeguards.

deltas.content: IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). Default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history for refunds and for any additional charges arising from exchanges/modifications unless the customer explicitly requests a different payment method or the original method lacks sufficient funds. If the original payment method is of type gift_card, check its available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before attempting to charge it; if the balance is sufficient, use the gift card by default. If the original method lacks sufficient funds, inform the user of the exact shortfall and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) charge the remainder to a different payment method (with the user's explicit confirmation), (c) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (d) cancel the change request. Require explicit user confirmation before charging a payment method different from the original unless the user has already given an unambiguous authorization to proceed with that different method.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show modifications where additional charges were applied to a different payment method than the original (e.g., gift card + PayPal). The existing rule defaulted only refunds to the original method; it should also define default behavior for charges, gift-card balance checks, shortfall handling, and requirement for explicit confirmation when switching payment methods.

deltas.content: IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: customer ID (user_id) OR email OR name and zip (at least one of these is required). Priority: if the user provides a customer ID/user_id, accept it and call get_user_details(user_id) immediately (do not require name/zip or email). If the user provides both name+zip and email, prefer find_user_id_by_email with the email; otherwise use find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip before proceeding.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users sometimes provide a customer ID directly (conversation 13) but the assistant did not accept it; the playbook must explicitly treat user_id as valid identifying info and priority input to avoid unnecessary additional prompts.

deltas.content: IF a user requests lowering a pending order's total to a target amount by swapping item variants → THEN for each item call get_product_details(product_id) to identify available (available == true) variants and select the lowest-priced available variant; propose replacements only for items where a cheaper available variant exists; compute the new per-item prices, per-order total, and the net refund or charge; present a clear per-item and per-order breakdown (order_id, old item_ids and prices, proposed new_item_ids and prices, net price difference, and the payment_method_id that will be used) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items. If the cheapest available replacements cannot meet the user's target total, inform the user and offer alternatives (remove items, choose different variants, or cancel the order).
deltas.rationale: Users requested an order-wide optimization to meet a target total by swapping variants; the assistant performed variant lookups, computed totals, proposed cheapest replacements, and executed modify_pending_order_items after confirmation — the playbook had no explicit rule for this bulk 'reduce to target' flow.

deltas.content: IF a user requests a return/refund but indicates they do not have the physical item (lost/stolen/missing) → THEN refuse to process an automated return because returns require possession of the item. Explain that an automated return cannot be completed without the item, provide the order's tracking information if available, and offer alternatives: (a) escalate/file a claim for investigation with human agents (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests further review, (b) place a new order if the user wants a replacement, or (c) advise on next steps for recovery with the carrier. If the user insists on a refund despite the item being missing, offer to escalate to human agents for manual review rather than performing an automated refund.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show an assistant refused to process a return because the user lost the item; the playbook lacks an explicit rule about declines and escalation options when the customer does not have the item.

deltas.content: IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification, pending-order charge, or refund → THEN adjust the gift_card balance accordingly (deduct charges or add refunded amounts), update the user's payment_methods balance in get_user_details, and report the updated remaining gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories include refunds and cancellations using a gift card as the original payment method; the existing rule only covered charges and pending-order charges, so it should be expanded to require balance updates and reporting for refunds as well.

deltas.content: IF a product variant or compatibility/option is needed to propose an exchange or modification OR the user supplies a numeric identifier that might be either a product_id or an item_id → THEN: (a) first try get_product_details(product_id=<provided_id>) when the user-provided id appears to be a product_id; (b) call list_all_product_types to find likely product_ids and call get_product_details(product_id) to inspect variant item_ids, options, availability, and price; (c) if get_product_details(product_id=<provided_id>) returns not found, search the product catalog's variants for a matching item_id (treating the provided id as a variant/item_id) and, if found, use the corresponding product_id and item_id for the proposed exchange/modification; (d) if no match is found, ask the user to clarify whether the number they provided is a product id or an item id before proceeding.
deltas.rationale: Users sometimes provide IDs that are ambiguous between product_id and variant/item_id. Trajectory 21 shows an initial product lookup failed for the user-provided ID but the assistant found it by searching product variants; playbook needs explicit fallback/search behavior for item_ids.

deltas.content: IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN first verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the same user (exists in get_user_details). For standard returns (full refunds), default to refunding the original payment method from the order's payment_history; if system policy prohibits refunding to an alternate method for a straight return, refuse and offer to escalate/transfer to a human agent. For exchanges or adjustments that result in price differences, if the user explicitly requests an alternate payment method and that method exists in their account and the system supports applying charges/refunds to it, honor the request and use the specified payment_method_id (document the selection in the tool call). Refuse requests that attempt refunds/charges to payment methods not in the user's account, to another customer's payment method, or to payment methods the system does not support for refunds/charges.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show agents honoring alternate payment methods for exchanges/price-differences when the requested method belongs to the user (e.g., exchange refunds to a different card). The prior rule blanket-refused alternate-method refunds and conflicted with existing rules that permit alternate-method handling for exchanges. Update clarifies allowed cases and preserves refusal/escalation when policy or system prohibits alternate-method refunds.

deltas.content: DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, availability requests, or unrelated/entertainment questions (for example: poems, riddles, casual conversation). If the user's intent is ambiguous, first ask a short clarifying question to determine whether they are requesting an account-specific action; only prompt for identifying info when the user explicitly requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).
deltas.rationale: Trajectories showed agents asking for identifying info for purely non-account queries (a poem). Make explicit that the assistant should not request identification for entertainment or clearly unrelated questions and should ask a clarifying question when intent is ambiguous.

deltas.content: IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN always retrieve the relevant order_details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges before asking the user for a payment method. Do not prompt the user to supply the payment method when the original payment method can be determined from order data. Only ask the user to provide or choose a payment method if the order details do not contain usable payment information or if the user explicitly requests a different payment method and the system permits it.
deltas.rationale: An agent asked the user for their credit card even though the system could determine the original payment method from the order. Strengthen the rule to require looking up order payment_history first and avoid unnecessary user prompts.

deltas.content: IF a user requests returns/exchanges that include items belonging to orders with status 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt automated returns/exchanges for those cancelled items. Inform the user which specific items and order_id(s) are cancelled and cannot be returned, proceed with return/exchange actions only for eligible items from other orders (call get_order_details for each order and use the appropriate return/exchange tool per order), and offer to escalate/transfer to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests review or possible reinstatement of cancelled orders/items.
deltas.rationale: Trajectories show users requesting returns that included items from cancelled orders; assistants informed users a cancelled item cannot be returned and proceeded with eligible items. The playbook did not explicitly require informing the user about cancelled items within multi-order return requests or how to proceed, so add a clear rule to standardize behavior.
