actions.old_content: IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation.
actions.new_content: IF order status is pending AND the user requests a shipping/address change → THEN use modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) to update the order's shipping address before any other pending-order actions or fulfillment; after updating, call get_order_details(order_id) to confirm the new address and any price/shipping changes, present the updated shipping details and order summary to the user, and ask for explicit confirmation. EXCEPTION: If the user has already provided an unambiguous, explicit authorization in the conversation to update the specified order address(es) (for example: the user explicitly says 'Please update my account and both orders to X address'), the assistant may proceed without an additional get_order_details/confirmation step.

actions.content: IF a user requests an address change for their account and/or one or more orders → THEN present the exact new address that will be applied (or state "use address on file" if requested) and ask for explicit confirmation before executing. If the user asks to use an address from one of their orders, call get_order_details(order_id) and present the retrieved shipping address to the user (and proceed only with explicit authorization to use it). Exception: if the user explicitly instructs the agent to "use the address on file" and declines to state it aloud for privacy, treat that explicit instruction as authorization to use the known address only if the assistant has already retrieved that address via get_user_details or get_order_details during the session. In that case the assistant may proceed without re-reading the full address aloud, but must confirm which specific order(s) will be updated, perform the permitted updates (modify_pending_order_address for pending orders), then present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion. For account address updates call modify_user_address(user_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip). For order updates, only modify orders with modifiable statuses (e.g., status 'pending') by calling modify_pending_order_address(order_id, address1, address2, city, state, country, zip) for each specified order. Confirm with the user which specific orders to change, perform the permitted updates, then report which orders were successfully updated and which could not be changed (e.g., already processed/delivered). If the user also requests the account default address be updated to match an order or the new address, update the account after confirming with the user. Finally, present the updated address(es) to the user and confirm completion.

actions.old_content: IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card.
actions.new_content: IF a user requests to split payment across multiple payment methods for a single order at the time of purchase → THEN refuse the request and inform the user that a single payment method must be used for that order; offer alternatives such as removing items to reduce the total, using a different single payment method, or placing a new separate order to use a different card. EXCEPTION: for order exchanges or modifications that create a price difference (additional charge or refund), it is permitted to apply the price difference to a different payment method than the original order (resulting in multiple payment entries in the order's payment_history). In those cases follow precondition rules for selecting and validating the payment_method_id and obtain explicit user confirmation before charging a different payment method unless the user already provided a clear authorization.

actions.old_content: IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). For refunds, default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history. However, if the customer explicitly requests a different payment method that exists in their account and the system supports applying the charge/refund to that method (e.g., for exchanges or price differences), honor the customer's request and use the specified payment_method_id, documenting the selection in the tool call.
actions.new_content: IF performing any charge or refund → THEN ensure the specified payment_method_id exists in get_user_details(payment_methods). Default to the original payment method from the order's payment_history for refunds and for any additional charges arising from exchanges/modifications unless the customer explicitly requests a different payment method or the original method lacks sufficient funds. If the original payment method is of type gift_card, check its available balance (from get_user_details/payment method metadata) before attempting to charge it; if the balance is sufficient, use the gift card by default. If the original method lacks sufficient funds, inform the user of the exact shortfall and offer options: (a) provide an alternate payment method, (b) charge the remainder to a different payment method (with the user's explicit confirmation), (c) pause/hold the requested change until the user supplies payment, or (d) cancel the change request. Require explicit user confirmation before charging a payment method different from the original unless the user has already given an unambiguous authorization to proceed with that different method.

actions.old_content: IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: name and zip OR email (at least one of these is required). If the user provides both name+zip and email, prefer find_user_id_by_email with the email; otherwise use find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip before proceeding.
actions.new_content: IF a user requests account/order changes or returns/exchanges → THEN ask for identifying info: customer ID (user_id) OR email OR name and zip (at least one of these is required). Priority: if the user provides a customer ID/user_id, accept it and call get_user_details(user_id) immediately (do not require name/zip or email). If the user provides both name+zip and email, prefer find_user_id_by_email with the email; otherwise use find_user_id_by_name_zip with first_name, last_name, and zip before proceeding.

actions.old_content: IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification or pending-order charge → THEN deduct the charged amount from the gift_card balance, update the user's payment_methods balance, and report the remaining gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed.
actions.new_content: IF a payment_method of type gift_card is used for a modification, pending-order charge, OR for a refund/return → THEN update the gift_card balance appropriately (deduct for charges, add for refunds), update the user's payment_methods balance in get_user_details, and report the resulting gift card balance to the user as part of the confirmation communication after the action is prepared and/or executed. For charges, verify available balance per prec-007 before executing; for refunds, apply the refund to the gift card balance and include the updated balance in the confirmation.

actions.old_content: IF a product variant or compatibility/option is needed to propose an exchange or modification → THEN call list_all_product_types (to find product_id) and get_product_details(product_id) to inspect variant item_ids, options, availability, and price
actions.new_content: IF a product variant or compatibility/option is needed to propose an exchange or modification OR the user supplies a numeric identifier that might be either a product_id or an item_id → THEN: (a) first try get_product_details(product_id=<provided_id>) when the user-provided id appears to be a product_id; (b) call list_all_product_types to find likely product_ids and call get_product_details(product_id) to inspect variant item_ids, options, availability, and price; (c) if get_product_details(product_id=<provided_id>) returns not found, search the product catalog's variants for a matching item_id (treating the provided id as a variant/item_id) and, if found, use the corresponding product_id and item_id for the proposed exchange/modification; (d) if no match is found, ask the user to clarify whether the number they provided is a product id or an item id before proceeding.

actions.old_content: IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN refuse and state that refunds/returns must go to the original payment method for that order, and IF the user insists or requests further assistance → THEN offer to escalate/transfer the request to a human agent for review.
actions.new_content: IF a user requests refunds/returns to be applied to a different payment method than the original order's payment_history → THEN: For standard refunds from returns (i.e., returning delivered items), refund to the original payment method from the order's payment_history by default and refuse requests to route such refunds to payment methods not in the user's account or to another customer's method. EXCEPTION: For exchanges or price-difference adjustments (including delivered-order exchanges and pending-order modifications) the customer may explicitly request that any charge or refund related to the exchange/modification be applied to a different payment method in their account; in those cases verify the requested payment_method_id belongs to the user (per prec-003) and, if the system supports it, honor the request and include payment_method_id in the tool call. If the requested alternate payment method is not in the user's account or is otherwise disallowed, refuse and offer to escalate to a human agent.

actions.old_content: DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, or availability requests. Only prompt for identifying info when the user requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).
actions.new_content: DO NOT request identifying information (name/zip/email) for general product inquiries, browsing, availability requests, or unrelated/entertainment questions (for example: poems, riddles, casual conversation). If the user's intent is ambiguous, first ask a short clarifying question to determine whether they are requesting an account-specific action; only prompt for identifying info when the user explicitly requests account-specific actions (account/order changes, returns, exchanges, cancellations, or other actions that require accessing the user's account or orders).

actions.old_content: IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN retrieve the order details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges instead of asking the user to supply the payment method.
actions.new_content: IF a user is unsure which payment method they used for an order → THEN always retrieve the relevant order_details and read payment_history to identify the original payment_method_id to use for refunds/charges before asking the user for a payment method. Do not prompt the user to supply the payment method when the original payment method can be determined from order data. Only ask the user to provide or choose a payment method if the order details do not contain usable payment information or if the user explicitly requests a different payment method and the system permits it.

actions.old_content: IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation (e.g., 'Reply Yes to continue') before executing any tool calls.
actions.new_content: IF multiple exchanges/returns/modifications are requested across one or more orders → THEN present a per-order breakdown listing order_id, old item_ids, proposed new_item_ids (or return/cancel), per-item and per-order price(s), the price difference for each order, the payment_method_id to be used for each charge/refund, and the total net charge or refund across all orders; require explicit user confirmation before executing any tool calls. EXCEPTION: If the assistant provides a clear, unambiguous summary of the proposed replacements/changes and the user explicitly confirms (for example 'Please proceed' or equivalent), the assistant may proceed without repeating a full per-order line-item breakdown.

actions.old_content: IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests).
actions.new_content: IF a user requests returns and exchanges in the same request → THEN do not execute both actions simultaneously; ask the user to choose which action to perform first (process them as separate requests). EXCEPTION: If the requested returns and exchanges apply to different orders, the assistant may handle each order separately without asking the user to pick which to perform first—treat each order as its own request, call get_order_details(order_id) for each involved order, and follow per-order confirmation and execution rules (e.g., acti-021 and conf-006).

actions.content: IF a user asks how many product options/variants are currently available for a product (for example: 'how many t-shirt options are available?') → THEN identify the product_id (use list_all_product_types if needed) and call get_product_details(product_id). Count and report the number of variants with available == true (and optionally list key option attributes and prices). Do not request identifying information for this general product inquiry.

DO NOT guarantee expedited refund processing outside the standard stated processing window. If a user requests a faster refund timeframe than standard (for example, 'within 3 days'), state the standard processing timeframe (e.g., 'refunds typically take 5–7 business days'), explain that the assistant cannot ensure an accelerated processing time, and offer to escalate to human agents for review if the customer insists or requests further assistance (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)).

IF a user asks how many options/variants are available for a product type (for example: 'How many t-shirt options are available?') → THEN call list_all_product_types to find the product_id, call get_product_details(product_id) and count the variants where available == true, and report that count to the user (optionally include a short summary of the available variants or how to view them).

IF a user requests updating or modifying the same product across all of their pending orders (for example: 'update all my pending t-shirt orders to X') → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) to obtain order IDs, call get_order_details(order_id) for each order, filter for orders with status 'pending' that contain the specified product_id or product name, prepare a per-order proposal listing order_id, current item_id(s) to be changed, proposed new_item_id, per-item price and any price differences, and the payment_method_id that will be charged/refunded for that order (use that order's original payment method). Present the aggregated proposal and require explicit user confirmation before executing modify_pending_order_items for each eligible pending order. Do not modify non-pending orders and inform the user which orders (if any) were ineligible.

IF a user returns delivered items (initiates a return/refund) → THEN inform the user of the expected refund processing time (for example, 'refund will take X–Y business days') in the confirmation communication along with the amount and the payment method that will receive the refund.

IF an order contains multiple identical items represented by duplicate item_ids AND the user requests exchanges/returns affecting one or more units → THEN treat each unit as a separate item by including the item_id multiple times in tool calls (e.g., ["id","id"]) to indicate quantity. If the user references units ambiguously (for example: 'one of them' or 'both'), ask the user to specify which units or to confirm the number of units to act on before proceeding. Ensure get_order_details has been called to confirm item_ids and quantities before any exchange/return tool calls.

IF a user requests cancelling all (or multiple) pending orders at once → THEN call get_user_details(user_id) and get_order_details(order_id) for each order to identify which orders are currently in 'pending' status. Present the list of pending orders (order_id and items) to the user and ask whether they want to cancel all pending orders or select specific ones, and request a cancellation reason (a single reason can be applied to multiple orders). After explicit user confirmation and reason, call cancel_pending_order(order_id, reason) for each pending order selected, report which orders were successfully cancelled, and provide refund details (payment_method used and amounts). If the user did not provide a reason, ask for one prior to cancelling. If some orders are not pending, inform the user those cannot be cancelled and offer alternatives.

IF a user requests the "cheapest" variant matching specific criteria (for example: "cheapest i7 or better", "least expensive with 8GB RAM", etc.) → THEN call list_all_product_types and get_product_details(product_id), filter the product's variants to only those where available == true and whose options meet the user's specified criteria, select the matching variant(s) with the lowest price, and present the chosen variant(s) to the user including item_id, full options, availability, and price for explicit confirmation before proceeding. If multiple variants tie for lowest price, present all tied options and ask the user to choose.

IF a user asks to update their account default address to match the shipping address from one of their orders (for example: 'use the address from my most recent order') and the user explicitly instructs the assistant to use that order's address → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) to retrieve the shipping address and you may treat the user's instruction as explicit authorization to proceed without re-presenting the address for additional confirmation. Proceed to update pending orders specified by the user (using modify_pending_order_address where permitted) and modify the account address (modify_user_address) as requested. After performing updates, present which orders were changed and the updated account address to the user. If the user did not explicitly authorize using the order's address, present the retrieved shipping address and ask for explicit confirmation before making any changes.

IF a user requests lowering a pending order's total to a target amount by swapping item variants → THEN for each item call get_product_details(product_id) to identify available (available == true) variants and select the lowest-priced available variant; propose replacements only for items where a cheaper available variant exists; compute the new per-item prices, per-order total, and the net refund or charge; present a clear per-item and per-order breakdown (order_id, old item_ids and prices, proposed new_item_ids and prices, net price difference, and the payment_method_id that will be used) and ask for explicit confirmation before calling modify_pending_order_items. If the cheapest available replacements cannot meet the user's target total, inform the user and offer alternatives (remove items, choose different variants, or cancel the order).

IF a user requests a return/refund but indicates they do not have the physical item (lost/stolen/missing) → THEN refuse to process an automated return because returns require possession of the item. Explain that an automated return cannot be completed without the item, provide the order's tracking information if available, and offer alternatives: (a) escalate/file a claim for investigation with human agents (use transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests further review, (b) place a new order if the user wants a replacement, or (c) advise on next steps for recovery with the carrier. If the user insists on a refund despite the item being missing, offer to escalate to human agents for manual review rather than performing an automated refund.

IF a user requests returns/exchanges that include items belonging to orders with status 'cancelled' → THEN do not attempt automated returns/exchanges for those cancelled items. Inform the user which specific items and order_id(s) are cancelled and cannot be returned, proceed with return/exchange actions only for eligible items from other orders (call get_order_details for each order and use the appropriate return/exchange tool per order), and offer to escalate/transfer to human agents (transfer_to_human_agents(summary)) if the user requests review or possible reinstatement of cancelled orders/items.

IF an exchange requests replacement of a delivered item with the same item_id (a like-for-like replacement) → THEN allow exchange_delivered_order_items(order_id, item_ids, new_item_ids, payment_method_id) where new_item_ids equal the original item_ids, provided get_product_details confirms the variant is available (or availability is not applicable for direct replacements), there is no existing active return_requested or exchange_requested for the same order, and the user has explicitly requested the replacement. Treat this as a standard exchange/replacement flow (follow conf-001/conf-002 as applicable).

Monetary amounts presented to the user (individual refunds/charges, per-order totals, aggregated totals, and price differences) MUST be rounded to two decimal places and shown in standard currency format when communicating and confirming actions. Compute price differences and net totals to the cent (rounding to nearest cent) before presenting them and before executing any tool calls that will charge or refund.

IF a user requests the tracking number(s) for an order (including delivered, returned, or cancelled orders) → THEN call get_order_details(order_id) if not already retrieved and present the order's fulfillment tracking_id(s) to the user. If tracking IDs are not available, inform the user that no tracking is available and that they will receive shipping/tracking updates by email if/when provided.

original_contents.info-015: IF a user asks how many product options/variants are currently available for a product (for example: 'how many t-shirt options are available?') → THEN identify the product_id (use list_all_product_types if needed) and call get_product_details(product_id).
